steps.title: Pin down the goal
steps.content: The student wants to identify the real author of Adam Bede. The answer must name the actual person who wrote the novel, not just describe the pseudonym situation.

steps.title: Find the key facts in the notes
steps.content: The notes establish that the title page said 'George Eliot,' that this was assumed to be a pseudonym, and that Mary Ann Evans later revealed herself as the real author. The correct choice must state that Mary Ann Evans wrote the novel.

steps.title: Eliminate by misread/overreach
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The real author of Adam Bede was Mary Ann Evans...': directly names the real author and explains the pseudonym, fulfilling the goal. ✓
  • Choice B — 'George Eliot... was widely assumed to be a pseudonym.': discusses the pseudonym status but never names the real author. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The title page... indicated that the author's name was George Eliot.': reports only the pseudonym from the title page, not the real author. ✗
  • Choice D — 'A woman who had used a pseudonym... later revealed herself as the real author.': confirms a woman revealed herself but never names her as Mary Ann Evans. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
